statement.markdown: Implement a binary search function in a sorted array that returns the index of the target element. If the target is not found, return -1.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a sorted array of integers and a target integer.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be the index of the target element or -1 if not found.
statement.notes: The array may contain duplicate elements.

constraints.description: The size of the input array
The target element to search for

examples.explanation: The target element 2 is found at index 3 in the sorted array.
The target element 6 is not found in the sorted array.

hints.text: Use a binary search approach to find the target element.
Consider the case when the target element is not found.

editorial.title: Binary Search Implementation
editorial.intuition: Use a binary search algorithm to find the target element in the sorted array.
editorial.bruteForce: Try to find the target element by iterating through the array.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a binary search approach to narrow down the search space.
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement a binary search function with a time complexity of O(log n).
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The binary search function will return the correct index of the target element or -1 if not found.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run with sample input.
commonMistakes: Not handling the case when the target element is not found.
Not considering the case when the target element is at the boundary of the array.

functionSignature.cpp: int binarySearch(int arr[], int n, int target);
functionSignature.java: public int binarySearch(int[] arr, int n, int target);
